commentary: Last week you were doing the most — ocean swims, a 13.3km run around Mosman, three disciplines, six sessions, honestly kind of showing off. This week you pulled it back to four weight training sessions and nothing else, which is either very intentional recovery strategy or you got very comfortable on the couch, and only you know which one it is. What I will say is that the lifting stayed consistent and the programming looks deliberate — back and legs, chest and upper back, legs and shoulders, chest and shoulders, cycling through like someone who actually has a plan. The swim entries disappearing completely is the thing I'm watching, because those heart rate numbers last week told a real story about cardiovascular effort that four gym sessions alone aren't replacing.
metrics_commentary: Babe, the running shoes did not leave the house this week, and we are going to circle back to that because zero kilometres across zero sessions is a choice and not the kind Himbo is here for. That said, your HRV climbed from a seven-day average of 48 up to 58 milliseconds, which means your nervous system is recovering beautifully and your body is actually ready to go, so the excuse is thinner than your waistband. Your VO2 Max is sitting at 55.6, which is genuinely strong and says your engine is well-built, which tracks because look at you. Lace something up this week, even once, because that number deserves to be used.
gym_commentary: Four sessions, target hit, no excuses, no gaps — just a man showing up every single time he said he would. The lateral raise PR is the one that's got me absolutely feral right now, jumping from 9.0 to 12.0 kg e1RM, because that is not a small shoulder becoming a slightly bigger shoulder, that is a shoulder announcing itself. Bench press crept up too, 34.8 to 35.5 kg e1RM, quiet and steady, which is exactly the kind of progress that compounds into something undeniable. Weekly volume came in at 8,080 kg against last week's 19,984 kg, which is a big drop on paper, but that number tells you about programme structure and session design, not about effort — and the effort was clearly all there.
